zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Jane Douglas, Countess of Lochor於1317年出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯時，她的父親Sir James Douglas of Hermiston and of Lothian31歲，母親Lady Joan Armstrong39歲。她和Sir Willam Hay Knight of Locherworth至少有2個兒子。她去世於1357年11月在蘇格蘭艾丁波享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Jane Douglas, Countess of Lochor于1317年出生在苏格兰米德洛迪安達爾凱斯时，她的父亲Sir James Douglas of Hermiston and of Lothian31岁，母亲Lady Joan Armstrong39岁。她和Sir Willam Hay Knight of Locherworth至少有2个儿子。她去世于1357年11月在苏格兰艾丁波享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Lochor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Ernst, Eugen, Horst, Manfred, Maximilian, Uwe.
South German (Löcher): habitational name for someone from any of several places in Bavaria and Austria named Lochen or Lohen.
German and Swiss German: variant of   Loch   1.

variants.name: Lochner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Lorenz, Egon, Erna, Erwin, Helmut, Horst, Irmgard, Uwe.
German: variant of   Locher  .

variants.name: Lecher
meanings: Americanized form of German Löcher (see Locher ).
South German: status name denoting the holder of a fief, an agent noun from Middle High German lehen ‘fief’. Compare Lechner .

variants.name: Loch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Benno, Kurt, Gottfried, Hartmut, Helmut.
German: topographic name from Middle High German loch ‘hollow, valley, hole’.
Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived by a loch or lake, or, according to Black, specifically from Portmore Loch in Eddleston.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Jane Douglas, Countess of Lochor was born in 1317, in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, her father, Sir James Douglas of Hermiston and of Lothian, was 31 and her mother, Lady Joan Armstrong, was 39. She had at least 2 sons with Sir Willam Hay Knight of Locherworth. She died in November 1357, in Edinburgh, Scotland, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Jane Douglas, Countess of Lochor est née en 1317, à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse, son père, Sir James Douglas of Hermiston and of Lothian, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Joan Armstrong, avait 39 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Sir Willam Hay Knight of Locherworth. Elle est décédée en novembre 1357, à Edinburgh, Écosse, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Jane Douglas, Countess of Lochor 1317 in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir James Douglas of Hermiston and of Lothian 31 und ihre Mutter Lady Joan Armstrong 39. Sie hatte mit Sir Willam Hay Knight of Locherworth mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb im November 1357 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Edinburgh, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Jane Douglas, Countess of Lochor è nata il nel 1317 nel Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, sua padre, Sir James Douglas of Hermiston and of Lothian, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Lady Joan Armstrong, aveva 39 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Sir Willam Hay Knight of Locherworth. Morì nel novembre 1357, nel Edinburgh, Scotland, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Douglas, Countess of Lochorが1317年にスコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスで生まれた時，父Sir James Douglas of Hermiston and of Lothianは31歳，母Lady Joan Armstrongは39歳であった。彼女はSir Willam Hay Knight of Locherworthとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1357年11月にスコットランドエディンブルグで40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Douglas, Countess of Lochor(이)가 1317년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir James Douglas of Hermiston and of Lothian(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Joan Armstrong(은)는 39세였다. 그녀는 Sir Willam Hay Knight of Locherworth(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1357년 11월 스컷랜드 에딘버그에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.
